items.summary: More than $10 billion will be spent bolstering Australia's fuel security as the federal government attempts to shield the nation from the long-term consequences of the war in Iran.

items.title: Afghanistan women's football player Mina Ahmadi says 'justice is served' after FIFA rule change
items.summary: A FIFA rule change has allowed the Afghan women's football team to compete in official internationals for the first time since the Taliban took over in 2021. Mina Ahmadi says the victory is a "very emotional" one for the team.

items.title: Commercial fishing restrictions in Gulf St Vincent extends for stock recovery
items.summary: One of South Australia's main fishing zones will remain closed for another year to allow stocks to recover from the algal bloom, sparking concerns from fishers over their future and warnings of price increases for consumers.

items.title: Freshwater turtles thriving only months after outback lake was on verge of drying up
items.summary: Hundreds of turtles are thriving near an outback NSW town, only months after the lake where they live was on the verge of drying up.

items.title: Turkish YouTuber describes the situation onboard MV Hondius
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: US influencer onboard MV Hondius during hantavirus outbreak
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Nadia Hernández presents living archive of Venezuelan protest music at AGNSW
items.summary: Unable to return to her homeland, Melbourne-based artist Nadia Hernández has reconnected with her culture through art.

items.title: Two Pacific Islander farm workers dead, one critical after Manjimup crash
items.summary: The men died in a vehicle crash near Manjimup in the state's South West.

items.title: Albury Hospital bed shortage hits hard ahead of Farrer by-election
items.summary: A major hospital is facing a significant bed shortage in the electorate of Farrer, where residents will soon vote in a by-election.

items.title: AFP warn of arrests on arrival for ISIS-linked families after Australia flights booked
items.summary: Flights have been booked for families linked to Islamic State fighters returning from Syria to Australia, with women in the group facing arrest and investigation after they arrive.

items.title: Tasmania's 30th governor announced as Caroline Wells, chair of Royal Flying Doctor Service Tasmania
items.summary: Caroline Wells will become the 30th Governor of Tasmania after receiving formal approval from King Charles III, replacing Barbara Baker AC.

items.title: What age should a child start swimming lessons?
items.summary: Swimming ability is declining in Australia, and experts say knowing the right age to enrol your children in lessons — as well as what to do if you can't access them — is key to improving water safety.

items.title: Arsenal reach Champions League final by beating Atletico Madrid to close in on historic double
items.summary: Arsenal reach the Champions League final for the second time in their history with a 1-0 home win over Atletico Madrid sealing a 2-1 aggregate victory in the semi-final.

items.title: Australian dollar surges past four-year high, ASX rallies 1.3pc — as it happened
items.summary: The local share market and currency rose strongly on Wednesday. Look back at how the session unfolded on the ABC News markets blog.

items.title: Project Freedom has been paused, so will it lead to a diplomatic breakthrough or reignite war with Iran?
items.summary: Iran had branded Project Freedom as "Project Deadlock" and now US president Donald Trump has placed the military's support mission in the Strait of Hormuz on hold.

items.title: Talia Gibson recovers from dreadful second set to beat Martina Trevisan in Rome
items.summary: Talia Gibson has made a fighting start on her debut on the clay of Rome, the Australian number two beating former world top-20 player Martina Trevisan to progress.

items.title: PM announces $10b fuel package, including government-owned reserve — as it happened
items.summary: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has announced that next week's federal budget will include a $10 billion fuel security package.

items.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby's family say they feel let down after missing her alleged murderer's court hearing
items.summary: The family of a five-year-old girl who died after going missing from an Alice Springs town camp last month say they feel let down by the system after missing her alleged killer's first court date.

items.title: Calls to protect culturally and linguistically diverse communities from 'anxiety-driven' NDIS cuts
items.summary: Nidhi Shekaran is one of thousands of NDIS participants waiting to learn if their services will be impacted as part of sweeping government reforms to the scheme.

items.title: Call for action on cold and flu products containing herb linked to anaphylaxis
items.summary: Andrographis paniculata is a common ingredient in cold and flu supplements, but it may not be as low-risk as first thought.

items.title: How do negative gearing, capital gains tax and trusts work?
items.summary: The government is poised to make changes to negative gearing, the capital gains tax discount, and the tax treatment of trusts in next week's federal budget. So how do they work, and what could the changes look like?

items.title: Families could save up to $50 a week growing their own fruit and vegetables
items.summary: With grocery prices continuing to bite, more Australians are discovering that home gardening isn't just good for wellbeing — it can also make real financial sense.

items.title: EV tax discount removed two days' worth of carbon emissions
items.summary: Two days' worth of carbon emissions were removed thanks to the $2 billion electric vehicle tax discount. But its supporters say that put 64,000 more EVs on the road, jump-starting the struggling industry.

items.title: Zelenskyy slams Russia's 'utter cynicism' as strikes kill 22 in Ukraine ahead of ceasefire
items.summary: The Ukrainian president called one attack in Poltava "especially vile", accusing Russia of launching a second missile while emergency rescuers were at the scene.

items.title: Farrer by-election could remake conservative politics in Australia
items.summary: The first electoral test for both new coalition party leaders, Angus Taylor and Matt Canavan, sees the opposition at serious risk of losing Farrer for the first time since the seat was created in 1949.

items.title: Businesses need to raise prices or put Australians out of work
items.summary: Australia had an inflation problem before the Iran war, but it's turned sinister. We've become hostage to oil prices, and as the governor of the Reserve Bank puts it: "We are all feeling poorer".

items.title: Interest rate rises push employed people and young families into mortgage stress
items.summary: Almost 14,000 Australians rang the National Debt Helpline last month, with most callers saying they are struggling to pay their home loans and are facing mortgage stress, according to new data.

items.title: More than 8 million people access income support including sick leave, driven by a growing number of mental health claims
items.summary: The number of people accessing income support payments has risen by 2 million in the past decade, with more Australians unable to work due to deteriorating mental and physical health.

items.title: 'Australia's first attractive comedian' Elouise Eftos looks for love in Aphrodite
items.summary: Last year, Elouise Eftos became the first Australian woman to land a best newcomer nod at Edinburgh Fringe. Now, she's got a new show about never falling in love.

items.title: How David Gee pulled off one of the most ambitious coin heists in Australian history
items.summary: David Gee pulled off one of the most baroque and ambitious coin heists in Australian history, nicking precious coins from the special collections of the nation's oldest library without anybody noticing.

items.title: Travel quiz, May 6: Fancy yourself a jetsetter? These questions will test you
items.summary: Which country does the alcoholic spirit cachaça hail from? And how much do you know about Australian towns with Big Koala statues?

items.title: Albanese government axes funding for $45 billion Inland Rail project linking Melbourne to Brisbane
items.summary: The federal government has halted plans for the Melbourne to Brisbane Inland Rail, amid concerns the project could cost more than $45 billion to complete.

items.title: Former AFL player's fight for compensation
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: 'Deeply offensive' video by comedian Alex 'Shooter' Williamson about Kumanjayi Little Baby condemned
items.summary: South Australia's Commissioner for Aboriginal Children and Young People speaks out against a "deeply offensive" social media video posted by comedian Alex "Shooter" Williamson in the wake of the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby.

items.title: Melbourne club operators in fear of threatening texts, kidnappings and arson attacks
items.summary: Melbourne is in the grip of a crime wave. But this time it's different and pub and club owners say they are scared as 'The Cartel' issues threats they do not understand.

items.title: Vietnam veteran John O'Halloran given Medal for Gallantry in Perth
items.summary: Vietnam veteran John O'Halloran is awarded Australia's third-highest military honour for leading a fixed bayonet charge on "one of Australia's worst days in South Vietnam".

items.title: South Sydney star Latrell Mitchell breaks his media ban to discuss form, PNG offers
items.summary: The NRL's strongest voice, Latrell Mitchell, breaks a lengthy league media ban to discuss his form, his positional switch and the potential of a move to PNG.

items.title: Complaints about work placements grow from Notre Dame nursing students and staff
items.summary: Numerous nursing students at Perth's Notre Dame University say issues with work placement have left them wondering if they will be able to graduate on time.

items.title: Raising interest rates won't lower petrol prices. Here's why the RBA did it anyway
items.summary: Sensible Emily untangles the latest interest rate decision and gives you tips to help you save money.

items.title: AFL fines Carlton $75,000 in wake of Elijah Hollands's mental health episode against Collingwood
items.summary: Three weeks on from Elijah Hollands's mental health episode at the MCG, the AFL fines Carlton $75,000 for its handling of the player and issues around the incident.

items.title: WA government defends decision to abandon emissions reduction targets
items.summary: The WA government concedes the state's greenhouse gas emissions may continue to rise, after the ABC revealed the government is abandoning plans to implement interim emissions reduction targets.

items.title: Reserve Bank lifts interest rates by another 0.25pc to 4.35pc
items.summary: The RBA lifts rates by 0.25 basis points to 4.35 per cent, which fully unwinds last year's rate cuts. But governor Michele Bullock says the rate rises will not stop higher inflation heading Australia's way from the Iran war.

items.title: Mortgage repayment calculator: How much will the RBA's latest rate hike cost you?
items.summary: Tell us your loan size, term and interest rate, and our mortgage repayment calculator will work out how much extra you may have to pay on your loan.

items.title: Calls for homelessness solutions intensify after death of baby in makeshift camp
items.summary: Community leaders in Wagga Wagga are demanding immediate action after a baby died and another was hospitalised during childbirth at a riverside camp.

items.title: Transport for NSW shortlists possible fixes to a failed section of the Great Western Highway
items.summary: Australian and international engineering firms are being shortlisted as they propose solutions to the failed section of the Great Western Highway.

items.title: Victorian government spruiks first surplus in seven years in 2026 budget
items.summary: The state government has failed to meaningfully tackle soaring debt, instead continuing to focus on cost-of-living programs and service delivery less than 200 days before voters head to the polls.

items.title: PM leaves door open to tax offset for workers as Coalition raises inflation concerns
items.summary: A one-off tax break for working Australians remains a live option for cost-of-living relief as Labor finalises plans for next week's federal budget.

items.title: Hawthorn ready to face Fremantle amid challenge from star ruckman Luke Jackson
items.summary: Hawthorn faces a major test on Thursday night against Fremantle in Perth as the Dockers sit on a seven-game winning streak.

items.title: Embracing being 'wasian' with a little help from Laufey
items.summary: Being biracial or "wasian" (that is, both white and Asian) is a unique experience. There are lots of things I share with both Asian and white people, but there are experiences novel to being wasian.

items.title: Monarto Safari Park hyena gets gold-coloured crown in dental treatment
items.summary: One of Monarto Safari Park's hyenas is flashing its new golden smile after a dental procedure to fix its deformed teeth and restore biting abilities.

items.title: Ex-AFL player Aiden O'Driscoll opens up on brain trauma hell and compensation fight
items.summary: Head trauma ended Aiden O'Driscoll's promising AFL career before he could play his first game. Now, like many former footballers affected by brain injuries, he faces an uncertain future as he fights for compensation.

items.title: These dads are putting down the tools and picking up a hair brush
items.summary: The viral social media trend encouraging dads to learn how to style their daughter's hair is challenging gender roles in country Australia.

items.title: Remote working encouraged at Surrey County Cricket Club to boost attendance at The Oval
items.summary: Working from home has become a mainstay of many economies since the COVID-19 pandemic. Surrey County Cricket Club figured that if people can work from home, maybe they can work from The Oval, too.

items.title: Met Gala 2026 red carpet: Beyoncé, Rihanna, Heidi Klum, Bad Bunny and Kylie Jenner
items.summary: A quick glance at the outfits from one of the fashion world's biggest events of the year.

items.title: Justin Baldoni and Blake Lively settle legal battle over It Ends With Us scandal
items.summary: The pair settle their civil case two weeks before a trial had been set to begin in New York.

items.title: RBA governor warns Australians to brace for inflation to get worse despite rate hikes — as it happened
items.summary: The RBA delivers another rate rise while the local share market closes lower amid escalating tension in the Middle East. Look back on how the day unfolded with our specialist business reporters on our blog.

items.title: French Open prize money raise criticised by Jannik Sinner and Aryna Sabalenka as not being enough
items.summary: Top players, including world number ones on the men's and women's tour, criticise the increased French Open prize money pool as not being enough amidst other unresolved welfare demands.

items.title: Separated couples forced to keep living together amid housing crisis
items.summary: Experts say more separated couples are continuing to stay under the same roof because housing is too expensive or scarce.

items.title: High Court to consider who owns rights to abattoir footage captured by activists' secret cameras
items.summary: A goat abattoir, a group of animal rights activists and the copyright act will be the focus today in a High Court challenge over who owns the rights to footage captured by secret cameras.

items.title: WA government preparing to abandon its emissions reduction targets, documents reveal
items.summary: The WA government is preparing to walk away from its own greenhouse gas emissions reduction targets and dramatically reshape how the state addresses climate change, the ABC can reveal.

items.title: Pharmacists turn patients away for home medicine reviews due to funding caps
items.summary: Pharmacists say government funding caps limiting the number of patients they can see in their homes for medication reviews means many are missing out on life-changing help.

items.title: Anglican bishop ordered to resign over relationship with priest appealing decision
items.summary: One of South Australia's most senior Anglicans is stripped of his position over an undisclosed relationship with a priest he ordained, in a case described by the church as "a gross ethical failure".

items.title: Supermarkets urged to absorb more price increases from Iran conflict
items.summary: As the price of food and agricultural production goes up during the Iran conflict, supermarkets are being urged to absorb more of the cost.

items.title: Push for free school lunch program to be adopted nationally
items.summary: Tasmania is "leading the way" with its largely state government-funded school lunch program. Now advocates are calling for a national rollout of the scheme, but warn parents may need to chip in.

items.title: What Jarome Luai's shock NRL move could mean for Australian and New Zealand rugby union
items.summary: The NRL's expansion has ramifications for more than the existing 17 clubs, with Australian and New Zealand rugby talent also in the crosshairs.
